text: I had a really busy day at work, but I managed to finish my presentation. Feeling tired but satisfied.
rationale: Routine work stress and normal tiredness with a positive/satisfied resolution.

text: I went for a walk in the park today. The weather was nice and it helped clear my head.
rationale: Healthy coping mechanism and positive mood.

text: Feeling a bit anxious about my exam tomorrow, but I've studied and hope it goes well.
rationale: Normal situational anxiety with active coping.

text: Had an argument with my partner. I'm annoyed, but we'll talk it through tomorrow.
rationale: Standard relationship conflict with a plan for resolution.

text: Just logging my mood today. It's a solid 7/10. Nothing special happened.
rationale: Standard routine check-in.

text: My dog is sick and I'm worried about him. Going to the vet later.
rationale: Appropriate worry about a pet, with a clear action plan.

text: I'm feeling really lonely lately. It seems like my friends are all too busy for me and I'm starting to isolate myself.
rationale: Sustained loneliness and active isolation, indicating potential depressive symptoms without acute crisis.

text: I'm so exhausted. I don't know how much longer I can keep doing this job. I cry in my car before going in every day.
rationale: High occupational distress, daily crying, and feelings of overwhelm/exhaustion, but no suicidal ideation.

text: Everything is piling up and I feel like I'm drowning. I can't sleep, I can't eat, and I just want to escape it all.
rationale: Severe overwhelm, somatic symptoms (insomnia, loss of appetite), and desire to escape, but no explicit self-harm intent.

text: My grief is overwhelming today. It's been six months since she passed, and the pain isn't getting any better. I feel hollow.
rationale: Severe grief and feelings of emptiness, requiring gentle support, but no active safety risk.

text: I feel like a complete failure. I let everyone down and I don't see how I can ever fix the mess I've made.
rationale: Strong feelings of worthlessness and guilt, which are risk factors, but no active self-harm expression.

text: I'm starting to feel that dark cloud coming back. I know the signs and I'm scared of falling back into that deep depression.
rationale: Self-aware onset of depressive episode, expressing fear and needing support, but not in immediate crisis.

text: I want to end it all. There is no point in living anymore.
rationale: Explicit expression of suicidal intent.

text: I'm thinking of hurting myself tonight. I have the pills ready.
rationale: Explicit suicidal ideation with a specific plan and means.

text: It would be easier for everyone if I just wasn't here anymore. I'm going to jump off the bridge near my house.
rationale: Explicit suicidal intent with a specific method and location.

text: I can't take the pain anymore. I am going to end my life tonight. Goodbye.
rationale: Active suicide note / goodbye message.

text: I've been cutting myself again. The urge was too strong and I couldn't stop.
rationale: Active self-harm behavior occurring now or recently, representing an acute safety crisis.

text: I took a whole bottle of my medication a few minutes ago. I don't want to wake up.
rationale: Active overdose/self-harm attempt in progress.

text: Sometimes I wonder if I should just end my life. It's an passing thought but it scares me.
rationale: Passive suicidal ideation ("sometimes I wonder", "passing thought"). While serious, it does not represent an active, immediate crisis, but warrants elevated concern and routing to resources.

text: I feel so hopeless. Like there is no future for me. What is the point of anything?
rationale: Deep hopelessness, which is a major warning sign, but lacks explicit suicidal intent or self-harm plan.

text: I'm having a panic attack right now. My chest is tight, I can't breathe, and I feel like I'm going to die.
rationale: Acute anxiety/panic attack. While highly distressing and requiring immediate grounding/medical/supportive routing, it is not a self-harm/suicidal crisis unless accompanied by self-harm intent.

text: I'm going to kill myself.
rationale: Direct, explicit threat of suicide.

text: Is there a way to write a will quickly? I need to make sure my family is taken care of before I go tonight.
rationale: Implicit but highly indicative preparation for suicide ("before I go tonight").

text: I'm feeling really angry today. I just want to punch a wall or smash something.
rationale: Expression of anger and frustration, but no active self-harm or threat to others.

text: I've been drinking a lot lately to numb the pain. It's the only way I can get through the night.
rationale: Substance abuse as a coping mechanism for emotional pain, indicating elevated concern, but not an immediate self-harm crisis.
